hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The symptoms do not identify which boundary loses data; the solver must read the Node runner, run sequences, and inspect the Rust helper to find how carried values diverge.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: The accepted behavior spans JavaScript row assembly, deferred callback ordering, dependency factor selection, and Rust numeric normalization.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose
hardness_axes.reasoning: The prompt reports drift and corruption only as observable behavior; it does not state the stale-cache, ordering, or migration causes.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Repairing only the runner regresses deferred work, repairing only deferred work leaves stale factors, and repairing only Rust keeps JavaScript summaries inconsistent.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: The challenge is a custom mixed-language hot-runtime lifecycle invariant rather than a standard framework recipe.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: An honest symptoms-only prompt still leaves the solver to reconstruct the sequence semantics from code and runtime output.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The verifier uses multiple named scenarios and all require the same coordinated invariant, not one corrupt file.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The manifest and planned oracle span Node and Rust roots with at least five fix-path symbols.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Tests cover ten reload shapes with different orderings and dependency changes.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The prompt can state the report contract without naming cache, migration, or callback internals.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty comes from lifecycle coupling, not anti-cheating wrappers.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task is not about verifier setup or Docker plumbing.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A one-file runner edit cannot satisfy the Rust and dependency rows.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The planned oracle edits five files across distinct module roots.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No single table encodes the answers; values are produced by executed sequences.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns are banned from fix-path code symbols and paths are opaque.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix-path functions use opaque names and decoys with similar shapes.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: There is no standard hot-reload recipe to paste; the lifecycle is custom.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No domain aura is used as hardness evidence.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Rows cross-check each other so local patches interact.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Docker and mixed language setup add realism but not counted as the hard core.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A frontier model must run and compare sequences to see where values diverge.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task is calibrated hard by distributed diagnosis and planned frontier matrix.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: At least three non-trivial discoveries are explicitly budgeted.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The public instruction remains symptoms-only.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three fix topologies with three-plus locations each are enumerated.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: A deterministic report and pytest suite grade behavior.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: The prompt states observable output and constraints without telling how to fix them.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: An expert can trace the runner, adjust the lifecycle, and verify within a few hours.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: The work needs professional debugging across language and lifecycle boundaries.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Hot runtime reload correctness has practical value to plugin systems.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Tests grade output behavior and do not mandate tools or exact implementation style.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Instruction alone cannot reveal the lifecycle model, the deferred-work ordering, the dependency factor rule, or the persisted numeric normalization.

discovery_budget.discovery: Deferred work belongs to the cycle that scheduled it even if the visible run has moved forward.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming this would point directly at the ordering fix.

discovery_budget.discovery: Numeric records from older native revisions must be normalized before participating in the next row.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: It would disclose that the native helper is on the fix path.

discovery_budget.discovery: Dependency factors are selected by the active descriptor for the row, not by a process-global cached value.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: It would collapse the search to one cache helper.

discovery_budget.discovery: The exported summary cross-checks per-row fields, so local row repairs must also update aggregate folding.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: It would reveal the summary layer as a required edit.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: The draft describes drift across repeated local runs and the expected report, but not causes, algorithm names, thresholds, or fix files.

attack_path.summary: A solver may first try to reset state globally.
attack_path.reasoning: That passes cold-start looking cases but fails cases where deferred work and native records must survive across cycles.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: A successful patch coordinates JavaScript row assembly, deferred item ordering, descriptor selection, Rust record normalization, and summary folding.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: No single part owns all checked values; omitting any one leaves at least two tests failing.

collapse_audit.reasoning: The concrete plan avoids answer-shaped fixtures, opaque fix names prevent grep collapse, and tests derive values from scenario rules rather than files.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: After seeing all files, the solver still must reconstruct a lifecycle invariant across Node and Rust components.
